exercises.title: Basic Swapping Simulator
tasks: Create 4-qubit state from two Bell pairs
Implement BSM projectors for middle qubits
Simulate measurement and obtain classical result
Apply Pauli corrections based on BSM outcome
Verify output is correct Bell state
hints: BSM projects R1R2 onto Bell basis
Corrections: I, X, Z, or XZ on Bob

exercises.title: Noisy Swapping Analyzer
exercises.description: Analyze swapping with noisy Werner state inputs.
tasks: Model Werner state inputs
Calculate theoretical output fidelity
Simulate many swap trials with random inputs
Generate fidelity vs input fidelity curves
Determine when swapping is beneficial
hints: Sample Bell states according to Werner probabilities
Track which output Bell state results

exercises.title: Nested Swapping Chain
tasks: Implement N-segment chain with 2^n segments
Perform log2(N) levels of swapping
Track fidelity through all levels
Calculate success probability with BSM efficiency
Compare with theoretical scaling
hints: Each level halves the number of pairs

exercises.title: Memory-Assisted Protocol
exercises.description: Implement swapping with quantum memory assistance.
tasks: Model quantum memory storage and retrieval
Simulate waiting for both segments to succeed
Include memory decoherence during waiting
Calculate effective fidelity vs memory coherence
Optimize memory cutoff time
hints: Store first pair while waiting for second
Trade-off: longer wait → more success, lower fidelity
Find optimal cutoff for maximum rate×fidelity
